criteria_checked.criterion_ref: Lawfulness of PLANNING the G4 override before the human gate
criteria_checked.note: planning the override pre-gate is LAWFUL and is in fact the ONLY lawful sequence: V.2/V.3's authorization chain (landmark-authorized scope.lock + human plan gate + critic/verifier chain) requires the override be stated in a DRAFT plan so the gate can ratify it; VIII.3(a) explicitly defers to this Art. V hatch. What would be unlawful: exercising the override before the gate, or wording implying the plan itself authorizes it. The Rev 2 wording is properly conditional ('under this plan's ... human plan gate'), except the 'pre-cleared' phrase (see blocker). Advisory: add explicit gate decision D6 ratifying the override, so the approval record unambiguously covers the blocking-verdict flip rather than relying on blanket plan approval.

advisory: '9/9 sub-gates PASS; G4 via the ... override ... if flagged' conflates two mechanisms -- G4 FAILs (and needs the env override); the landmark-flag sub-gate FLAGs (and needs nothing, FLAG never degrades). Split the clause: 'G4 green via the V.2-authorized DMC_GATE_PROTECTED override; landmark-flag raises RGATE-LANDMARK-FLAG (expected, non-degrading)'.

blockers.statement: AC1's dmc-glm-smoke clause is still literally unsatisfiable, now by self-reference: this cycle's OWN governance artifacts -- .harness/plans/dmc-stray-hygiene.md (dozens of dmc-glm-smoke occurrences), .harness/evidence/dmc-stray-hygiene-critic-r1.json, -r2.json, and the eventual .harness/verification/ report -- will be COMMITTED per established practice (the m10 plan and its critic-r1..r3 verdicts are tracked today), so the post-commit whole-tree `git grep -nF dmc-glm-smoke` REQUIRED by the Verification Commands row will return a class absent from the Findings enumeration (a)-(f), and AC1's 'returns ONLY the enumerated inert residuals' fails on literal reading. Note the grep's outcome flips across the recipe's own stages (pre-commit git grep skips the still-untracked plan; post-commit it does not) -- exactly the trap class of r1 BL-AC1. Fix (one line): add class (g) 'this cycle's own governance artifacts (plan, critic verdicts, verification report, closure evidence)' to the Findings enumeration and AC1, or scope the grep-diff with ':!.harness/' and state that .harness/ records are categorically historical/cycle prose.

blockers.statement: The G4 Finding's sentence 'the independent landmark-flag sub-gate stays armed and is pre-cleared by the landmark-authorized grants' misstates the enforcement machine (VI.2: machine source wins over prose). Verified against bin/lib/dmc-release-gate.py: sg_landmark (:617-636) never reads landmark_authorized or any scope.lock grant -- it unconditionally returns V_FLAG when new changes intersect non-ordinary landmarks, and 'FLAG never degrades the verdict' by frozen design (:14, :636, self-test U4 :973). Nothing 'pre-clears' the flag; it WILL raise RGATE-LANDMARK-FLAG on dmc-glm-smoke/bin-lib-repo-intel/MILESTONES and stay raised as a non-degrading review flag. Since this sentence is the core Art. V lawfulness narrative the human gate is asked to ratify, an approval gathered on a false mechanism description is a tainted authorization, and 'pre-cleared' invites a worse failure mode: an executor trying to make the expected FLAG disappear (= suppression, VIII.3(a)). Replace with mechanism-accurate wording, e.g.: 'the landmark-flag sub-gate stays armed and WILL raise RGATE-LANDMARK-FLAG on these paths -- expected and non-degrading by frozen composer design (FLAG never degrades the verdict); the authorization for the underlying edits lives in the landmark-authorized scope.lock + this plan's human gate (v1.0.1 precedent), not in any clearing of the flag.'
blockers.evidence_ref: bin/lib/dmc-release-gate.py:14,617-636,973 vs plan :110-113; grep 'landmark_authorized' over dmc-release-gate.py (no hit in sg_landmark)
